headline_summary: Reuters: US-Iran announce two-week ceasefire via Pakistan mediation, but hostilities and regional spillovers continue (incl. Lebanon/Gulf strikes).
Reuters: Strait of Hormuz remains conditional; Iran-linked naval threats keep war-risk and freight elevated despite some transits.
Reuters: Oil fell below $100 after ceasefire headline but remains well above pre-conflict levels; renewed spikes possible if fighting resumes or blockade persists.
Reuters: Iran sets preconditions for lasting peace talks (halt strikes, guarantees, compensation) and signals intent to levy fees for Hormuz passage.
Broader Middle East security environment remains brittle: maritime chokepoints, energy infrastructure, and proxy fronts create tail-risk for supply and risk assets.
risk_drivers: Ceasefire status unclear; active fighting and retaliation risk remains
Strait of Hormuz access is conditional and could tighten again quickly
Cross-border spillovers (Lebanon/Hezbollah front and Gulf energy infrastructure targeting)
Policy uncertainty around sanctions, shipping fees, and enforcement
High sensitivity of oil/LNG prices and insurance to headline risk

Energy: oil_up_risk: any ceasefire breakdown or renewed Hormuz restrictions could re-price crude higher quickly
Physical differentials, freight and war-risk insurance likely remain volatile while transit is conditional
LNG and refined product balances in the Gulf remain vulnerable to drone/missile risk
FX: em_fx_pressure: energy-importer EM currencies face renewed pressure if crude rebounds; current-account and inflation channels
risk_off_flows: USD/CHF/JPY benefit on escalation headlines; higher vol for high-beta FX
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: risk-off widens EM/HY spreads; oil-driven inflation risk can steepen breakevens
If ceasefire holds and oil stays sub-$100, near-term inflation impulse may ease vs late-March peaks
Equities: risk_off_flows: airlines/shipping/consumer cyclicals most exposed to higher fuel and freight; relative support for energy/defense
GCC equities likely volatile: higher oil helps fiscal, but infrastructure/security risk weighs on multiples

sources.title: Explainer: US-Iran ceasefire: what we know
Iran sets preconditions for talks on lasting peace with U.S., senior official tells Reuters

events.title: U.S. and Iran agree to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan; fighting and regional spillovers continue
events.implication: Ceasefire headline reduces immediate escalation risk but does not remove tail-risk; markets may fade risk premium if it holds, but pricing remains headline-driven.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively conditional despite ceasefire; Iranian navy threats reported by shipping sources
events.implication: Even partial restrictions sustain war-risk premia in crude, freight and insurance; re-routing and delays can tighten physical supply.

events.title: Iran sets preconditions for lasting peace talks with the U.S., including halt to strikes, guarantees, and compensation
events.implication: Raises negotiation complexity and timing risk; prolongs uncertainty around sanctions, shipping access, and energy flows.

events.title: Iran signals intent to levy fees for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz as part of a permanent peace framework
events.implication: Would raise structural transport costs and could reprice regional crude differentials; also increases sanction/evasion and compliance complexity.

links.from: U.S. and Iran agree to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan; fighting and regional spillovers continue
links.why: Ceasefire headline can ease acute risk-off, but Reuters notes ongoing fighting and spillovers keep markets headline-sensitive.

links.from: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively conditional despite ceasefire; Iranian navy threats reported by shipping sources
links.why: Reuters reports threats and conditional passage; this directly constrains a major oil chokepoint and tightens supply expectations.

links.from: Iran signals intent to levy fees for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz as part of a permanent peace framework
links.why: Passage fees would increase transport costs and risk premia even without outright closure.

links.from: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively conditional despite ceasefire; Iranian navy threats reported by shipping sources
links.why: Higher/volatile oil and freight costs typically pressure energy-importer EM FX through trade balance and inflation channels.

links.from: Iran sets preconditions for lasting peace talks with the U.S., including halt to strikes, guarantees, and compensation
links.why: More demanding terms can slow a durable deal, sustaining risk premia that can widen credit spreads.
